records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on Judging by the Testimony of One Witness and the Oath of the Claimant
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3319 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Al-Alaa, from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said: "If the matter were up to us, we would permit the testimony of one man, if he is known for goodness, along with the oath of the claimant, in the rights of people. However, as for the rights of Allah (swt), the Almighty, and the sighting of the crescent moon, we would not permit it."
records.arabicText: 3319 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنِ اَلْعَلاَءِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "لَوْ كَانَ اَلْأَمْرُ إِلَيْنَا لَأَجَزْنَا شَهَادَةَ اَلرَّجُلِ إِذَا عُلِمَ مِنْهُ خَيْرٌ مَعَ يَمِينِ اَلْخَصْمِ فِي حُقُوقِ اَلنَّاسِ فَأَمَّا مَا كَانَ مِنْ حُقُوقِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ رُؤْيَةِ اَلْهِلاَلِ فَلاَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3319 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Al-Alaa, from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If the matter were up to us, we would permit the testimony of one man, if he is known for goodness, along with the oath of the claimant, in the rights of people. However, as for the rights of Allah (swt), the Almighty, and the sighting of the crescent moon, we would not permit it."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on Judging by the Testimony of Two Women and the Oath of the Claimant
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3320 - Mansur ibn Hazim narrated that Abu al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as), said: "If two women testify in favor of the claimant along with his oath, it is permissible."
records.arabicText: 3320 - رَوَى مَنْصُورُ بْنُ حَازِمٍ أَنَّ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ مُوسَى بْنَ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "إِذَا شَهِدَ لِطَالِبِ اَلْحَقِّ اِمْرَأَتَانِ وَ يَمِينَهُ فَهُوَ جَائِزٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3320 - Mansur ibn Hazim narrated that Abu al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as), said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If two women testify in favor of the claimant along with his oath, it is permissible."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on Judging by the Testimony of Two Women and the Oath of the Claimant
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3321 - Hammad narrated from al-Halabi, from Abu Abdullah (as), that:
"The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace be upon him and his family, permitted the testimony of women along with the oath of the claimant in matters of debt, where he swears by Allah (swt) that his claim is true."
records.arabicText: 3321 - وَ رَوَى حَمَّادٌ عَنِ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "أَنَّ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ أَجَازَ شَهَادَةَ اَلنِّسَاءِ مَعَ يَمِينِ اَلطَّالِبِ فِي اَلدَّيْنِ يَحْلِفُ بِاللَّهِ إِنَّ حَقَّهُ لَحَقٌّ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3321 - Hammad narrated from al-Halabi, from Abu Abdullah (as), that:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace be upon him and his family, permitted the testimony of women along with the oath of the claimant in matters of debt, where he swears by Allah (swt) that his claim is true."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on Establishing Testimony Based on Knowledge Without Formal Witnessing
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3322 - Al-Ala narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), regarding a man who observes the accounting between two men and is then called to testify.
Imam (as) said: "If he wishes, he may testify, and if he wishes, he may abstain from testifying."
records.arabicText: 3322 - رَوَى اَلْعَلاَءُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ اَلْبَاقِرِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اَلرَّجُلِ يَشْهَدُ حِسَابَ اَلرَّجُلَيْنِ ثُمَّ يُدْعَى إِلَى اَلشَّهَادَةِ قَالَ "إِنْ شَاءَ شَهِدَ وَ إِنْ شَاءَ لَمْ يَشْهَدْ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3322 - Al-Ala narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), regarding a man who observes the accounting between two men and is then called to testify.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If he wishes, he may testify, and if he wishes, he may abstain from testifying."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on Establishing Testimony Based on Knowledge Without Formal Witnessing
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3323 - Ibn Faddal narrated from Ahmad ibn Yazid, from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), regarding a man who observes the accounting between two men and is then called to testify.
Imam (as) said: "He should testify."
records.arabicText: 3323 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ فَضَّالٍ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ يَزِيدَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اَلرَّجُلِ يَشْهَدُ حِسَابَ اَلرَّجُلَيْنِ ثُمَّ يُدْعَى إِلَى اَلشَّهَادَةِ قَالَ "يَشْهَدُ".

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on Establishing Testimony Based on Knowledge Without Formal Witnessing
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3324 - Ali ibn Ahmad ibn Ashyam narrated that he asked Abu al-Hasan, peace be upon him, about a man whose wife became pure from her menstruation, and he said: "So-and-so is divorced," while people were listening to his words, but he did not tell them, "Bear witness." Does the divorce take effect upon her?
Imam (as) said: "Yes, this is a testimony. Would you leave her in suspense?"
records.arabicText: 3324 - وَ رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَشْيَمَ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ طَهُرَتِ اِمْرَأَتُهُ مِنْ حَيْضِهَا فَقَالَ فُلاَنَةُ طَالِقٌ وَ قَوْمٌ يَسْمَعُونَ كَلاَمَهُ وَ لَمْ يَقُلْ لَهُمُ اِشْهَدُوا أَ يَقَعُ اَلطَّلاَقُ عَلَيْهَا قَالَ "نَعَمْ هَذِهِ شَهَادَةٌ أَ فَتَتْرُكُهَا مُعَلَّقَةً".

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on Establishing Testimony Based on Knowledge Without Formal Witnessing
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3325 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), said:
"Knowledge is a testimony when its possessor is wronged."
records.arabicText: 3325 - فَقَدْ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "اَلْعِلْمُ شَهَادَةٌ إِذَا كَانَ صَاحِبُهُ مَظْلُوماً".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3325 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Knowledge is a testimony when its possessor is wronged."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on Refraining From Testimony, What Has Been Mentioned About Establishing it, Emphasizing it, and Concealing it
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3326 - It is narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Fudayl who said that the Righteous Servant, peace be upon him, said: "One who is called to bear witness should not refrain from it."
records.arabicText: 3326 - رُوِيَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ اَلْفُضَيْلِ قَالَ اَلْعَبْدُ اَلصَّالِحُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "لاَ يَنْبَغِي لِلَّذِي يُدْعَى إِلَى شَهَادَةٍ أَنْ يَتَقَاعَسَ عَنْهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3326 - It is narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Fudayl who said that the Righteous Servant, peace be upon him, said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "One who is called to bear witness should not refrain from it."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on Refraining From Testimony, What Has Been Mentioned About Establishing it, Emphasizing it, and Concealing it
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3327 - It is narrated from Hisham ibn Salim, from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding the words of Allah (swt), the Almighty and Majestic: "And the witnesses should not refuse when they are called" (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:282)
Imam (as) said: "This refers to before giving testimony."
And regarding His (swt) words: "And whoever conceals it, his heart is indeed sinful" (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:283)
Imam (as) said: "This refers to after giving testimony."
records.arabicText:
3327 - وَ رَوَى هِشَامُ بْنُ سَالِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي قَوْلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ:
۝  وَ لاٰ يَأْبَ اَلشُّهَدٰاءُ إِذٰا مٰا دُعُوا ۝ قَالَ "قَبْلَ اَلشَّهَادَةِ"
وَ فِي قَوْلِهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ:
۝ وَ مَنْ يَكْتُمْهٰا فَإِنَّهُ آثِمٌ قَلْبُهُ ۝ قَالَ "بَعْدَ اَلشَّهَادَةِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3327 - It is narrated from Hisham ibn Salim, from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding the words of Allah (swt), the Almighty and Majestic: "And the witnesses should not refuse when they are called" (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:282)
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Imam (as) said: "This refers to before giving testimony."
And regarding His (swt) words: "And whoever conceals it, his heart is indeed sinful" (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:283)
Imam (as) said: "This refers to after giving testimony."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on Refraining From Testimony, What Has Been Mentioned About Establishing it, Emphasizing it, and Concealing it
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3328 - It is narrated from Uthman ibn Isa, from some of our companions, from Abu Abdullah (as). I said to Imam (as), "A man from my brothers has a testimony with me, but not all of it is admissible by the judges here."
Imam (as) said: "If you know that it is true, then validate it in every possible way so that his right is established."
records.arabicText: 3328 - وَ رَوَى عُثْمَانُ بْنُ عِيسَى عَنْ بَعْضِ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لَهُ يَكُونُ لِلرَّجُلِ مِنْ إِخْوَانِي عِنْدِي اَلشَّهَادَةُ لَيْسَ كُلُّهَا تُجِيزُهَا اَلْقُضَاةُ عِنْدَنَا قَالَ "إِذَا عَلِمْتَ أَنَّهَا حَقٌّ فَصَحِّحْهَا بِكُلِّ وَجْهٍ حَتَّى يَصِحَّ لَهُ حَقُّهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3328 - It is narrated from Uthman ibn Isa, from some of our companions, from Abu Abdullah (as).
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I said to Imam (as), "A man from my brothers has a testimony with me, but not all of it is admissible by the judges here."
Imam (as) said: "If you know that it is true, then validate it in every possible way so that his right is established."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on Refraining From Testimony, What Has Been Mentioned About Establishing it, Emphasizing it, and Concealing it
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3329 - It is narrated from Jabir, from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said that the Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said: "Whoever conceals testimony or gives false testimony to shed the blood of a Muslim or to destroy the wealth of a Muslim, will come on the Day of Judgment with darkness on his face extending as far as the eye can see, and scars on his face by which all creatures will recognize him by his name and lineage. And whoever testifies truthfully to preserve the wealth of a Muslim, will come on the Day of Judgment with light on his face extending as far as the eye can see, and all creatures will recognize him by his name and lineage."
Then Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), said: "Do you not see that Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious, says: 'And establish the testimony for Allah (swt)'?" (Surah At-Talaq 65:2)
records.arabicText: 3329 - وَ رَوَى جَابِرٌ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "مَنْ كَتَمَ اَلشَّهَادَةَ أَوْ شَهِدَ بِهَا لِيُهْدِرَ بِهَا دَمَ اِمْرِئٍ مُسْلِمٍ أَوْ لِيُتْوِيَ مَالَ اِمْرِئٍ مُسْلِمٍ أَتَى يَوْمَ اَلْقِيَامَةِ وَ لِوَجْهِهِ ظُلْمَةٌ مَدَّ اَلْبَصَرِ وَ فِي وَجْهِهِ كُدُوحٌ تَعْرِفُهُ اَلْخَلاَئِقُ بِاسْمِهِ وَ نَسَبِهِ وَ مَنْ شَهِدَ شَهَادَةَ حَقٍّ لِيُحْيِيَ بِهَا مَالَ اِمْرِئٍ مُسْلِمٍ أَتَى يَوْمَ اَلْقِيَامَةِ وَ لِوَجْهِهِ نُورٌ مَدَّ اَلْبَصَرِ تَعْرِفُهُ اَلْخَلاَئِقُ بِاسْمِهِ وَ نَسَبِهِ" ثُمَّ قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ "أَ لاَ تَرَى أَنَّ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ يَقُولُ: ۝ وَ أَقِيمُوا اَلشَّهٰادَةَ لِلّٰهِ ۝ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3329 - It is narrated from Jabir, from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said that the Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Whoever conceals testimony or gives false testimony to shed the blood of a Muslim or to destroy the wealth of a Muslim, will come on the Day of Judgment with darkness on his face extending as far as the eye can see, and scars on his face by which all creatures will recognize him by his name and lineage. And whoever testifies truthfully to preserve the wealth of a Muslim, will come on the Day of Judgment with light on his face extending as far as the eye can see, and all creatures will recognize him by his name and lineage."
Then Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), said: "Do you not see that Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious, says: 'And establish the testimony for Allah (swt)'?" (Surah At-Talaq 65:2)

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on Refraining From Testimony, What Has Been Mentioned About Establishing it, Emphasizing it, and Concealing it
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3330 - And Imam (as) said regarding the saying of Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious: "And whoever conceals it, then indeed his heart is sinful" (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:283)
Imam (as) said: "His heart is disbelieving."
records.arabicText:
3330 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي قَوْلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ: ۝ وَ مَنْ يَكْتُمْهٰا فَإِنَّهُ آثِمٌ قَلْبُهُ ۝
قَالَ "كَافِرٌ قَلْبُهُ".

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on False Testimony and What Has Been Reported Regarding it
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3331 - Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Jamil ibn Darraj from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding false testimony.
Imam (as) said: "If the thing is present in its original form, it is returned to its owner. But if it is no longer present, then the false witness is liable for the value of what he caused to be lost from the man's property."
records.arabicText: 3331 - رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ جَمِيلِ بْنِ دَرَّاجٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي شَهَادَةِ اَلزُّورِ قَالَ "إِذَا كَانَ اَلشَّيْءُ قَائِماً بِعَيْنِهِ رُدَّ عَلَى صَاحِبِهِ وَ إِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ قَائِماً ضَمِنَ بِقَدْرِ مَا أَتْلَفَ مِنْ مَالِ اَلرَّجُلِ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3331 - Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Jamil ibn Darraj from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding false testimony.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If the thing is present in its original form, it is returned to its owner. But if it is no longer present, then the false witness is liable for the value of what he caused to be lost from the man's property."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on False Testimony and What Has Been Reported Regarding it
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3332 - Sama'ah narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said: "False witnesses are subjected to flogging as a punishment, and its extent is determined by the Imam. They are paraded publicly so that they may be recognized and refrain from repeating the act."
I said: "If they repent and reform, will their testimony be accepted afterward?"
Imam (as) said: "If they repent, Allah (swt) accepts their repentance, and their testimony will be accepted thereafter."
records.arabicText: 3332 - وَ رَوَى سَمَاعَةُ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "شُهُودُ اَلزُّورِ يُجْلَدُونَ حَدّاً وَ لَيْسَ لَهُ وَقْتٌ ذَلِكَ إِلَى اَلْإِمَامِ وَ يُطَافُ بِهِمْ حَتَّى يُعْرَفُوا وَ لاَ يَعُودُوا " قَالَ قُلْتُ فَإِنْ تَابُوا وَ أَصْلَحُوا أَ تُقْبَلُ شَهَادَتُهُمْ بَعْدُ فَقَالَ "إِذَا تَابُوا تَابَ اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ وَ قُبِلَتْ شَهَادَتُهُمْ بَعْدُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3332 - Sama'ah narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"False witnesses are subjected to flogging as a punishment, and its extent is determined by the Imam. They are paraded publicly so that they may be recognized and refrain from repeating the act."
I said: "If they repent and reform, will their testimony be accepted afterward?"
Imam (as) said: "If they repent, Allah (swt) accepts their repentance, and their testimony will be accepted thereafter."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on False Testimony and What Has Been Reported Regarding it
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3333 - It is reported that when Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) apprehended a false witness, if the person was a stranger, he would send him back to his tribe. If the person was a tradesman, Imam (as) would send him to his marketplace. Then Imam (as) would parade him publicly, imprison him for a few days, and afterward release him.
records.arabicText: 3333 - وَ كَانَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ إِذَا أَخَذَ شَاهِدَ زُورٍ فَإِنْ كَانَ غَرِيباً بَعَثَ بِهِ إِلَى حَيِّهِ وَ إِنْ كَانَ سُوقِيّاً بَعَثَ بِهِ إِلَى سُوقِهِ ثُمَّ يُطِيفُ بِهِ ثُمَّ يَحْبِسُهُ أَيَّاماً ثُمَّ يُخَلِّي سَبِيلَهُ.

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on False Testimony and What Has Been Reported Regarding it
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3334 - Ibrahim ibn Abdul Hamid reported from Abu Basir, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding a woman for whom two witnesses testified that her husband had died. She then remarried, but later her first husband returned.
Imam (as) said: "The second husband owes her the dowry because he lawfully enjoyed her intimacy. The two witnesses are to be given the prescribed punishment (for false testimony) and held liable for the dowry due to having deceived the man. The woman must observe the waiting period ('iddah) and then return to her first husband."
records.arabicText: 3334 - وَ رَوَى إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اَلْحَمِيدِ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اِمْرَأَةٍ شَهِدَ عِنْدَهَا شَاهِدَانِ بِأَنَّ زَوْجَهَا مَاتَ فَتَزَوَّجَتْ ثُمَّ جَاءَ زَوْجُهَا اَلْأَوَّلُ قَالَ "لَهَا اَلْمَهْرُ بِمَا اِسْتَحَلَّ مِنْ فَرْجِهَا اَلْأَخِيرُ وَ يُضْرَبُ اَلشَّاهِدَانِ اَلْحَدَّ وَ يُضَمَّنَانِ اَلْمَهْرَ بِمَا غَرَّا اَلرَّجُلَ ثُمَّ تَعْتَدُّ وَ تَرْجِعُ إِلَى زَوْجِهَا اَلْأَوَّلِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3334 - Ibrahim ibn Abdul Hamid reported from Abu Basir, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding a woman for whom two witnesses testified that her husband had died. She then remarried, but later her first husband returned.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The second husband owes her the dowry because he lawfully enjoyed her intimacy. The two witnesses are to be given the prescribed punishment (for false testimony) and held liable for the dowry due to having deceived the man. The woman must observe the waiting period ('iddah) and then return to her first husband."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on False Testimony and What Has Been Reported Regarding it
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3335 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub reported from Al-Ala and Abu Ayyub, who narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), regarding two men who testified against an absent man in the presence of his wife, claiming that he had divorced her.
The woman observed her waiting period (iddah) and then remarried. Later, the absent husband returned, asserting that he had not divorced her, and one of the witnesses admitted to lying.
Imam (as) said: "The second husband has no claim over her, and the dowry is to be taken from the witness who retracted his testimony and returned to the second husband. They must be separated, and the woman must observe the waiting period (iddah) from the second husband. The first husband may not approach her until her waiting period has ended."
records.arabicText: 3335 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنِ اَلْعَلاَءِ وَ أَبِي أَيُّوبَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلَيْنِ شَهِدَا عَلَى رَجُلٍ غَائِبٍ عِنْدَ اِمْرَأَتِهِ بِأَنَّهُ طَلَّقَهَا فَاعْتَدَّتِ اَلْمَرْأَةُ وَ تَزَوَّجَتْ ثُمَّ إِنَّ اَلزَّوْجَ اَلْغَائِبَ قَدِمَ فَزَعَمَ أَنَّهُ لَمْ يُطَلِّقْهَا وَ أَكْذَبَ نَفْسَهُ أَحَدُ اَلشَّاهِدَيْنِ فَقَالَ "لاَ سَبِيلَ لِلْأَخِيرِ عَلَيْهَا وَ يُؤْخَذُ اَلصَّدَاقُ مِنَ اَلَّذِي شَهِدَ وَ رَجَعَ فَيُرَدُّ عَلَى اَلْأَخِيرِ وَ يُفَرَّقُ بَيْنَهُمَا وَ تَعْتَدُّ مِنَ اَلْأَخِيرِ وَ لاَ يَقْرَبُهَا اَلْأَوَّلُ حَتَّى تَنْقَضِيَ عِدَّتُهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3335 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub reported from Al-Ala and Abu Ayyub, who narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), regarding two men who testified against an absent man in the presence of his wife, claiming that he had divorced her.
records.thaqalaynMatn:
The woman observed her waiting period (iddah) and then remarried. Later, the absent husband returned, asserting that he had not divorced her, and one of the witnesses admitted to lying.
Imam (as) said: "The second husband has no claim over her, and the dowry is to be taken from the witness who retracted his testimony and returned to the second husband. They must be separated, and the woman must observe the waiting period (iddah) from the second husband. The first husband may not approach her until her waiting period has ended."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on False Testimony and What Has Been Reported Regarding it
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3336 - Ali ibn Matar narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, who reported from Abu Abdullah (as), that Imam (as) said: "False witnesses are subjected to a punishment of flogging, the duration of which is determined by the Imam. They are paraded so that people recognize them." Allah (swt), the Almighty, says: 'And do not accept their testimony ever, and those are the defiantly disobedient-except for those who repent.' (Surah An-Nur 24:4-5)
I asked: 'How is their repentance recognized?'
Imam (as) said: 'He must publicly declare his falsehood before the witnesses where he was punished, and seek forgiveness from his Lord (azj), the Almighty. If he does this, then his repentance becomes evident.'"
records.arabicText:
3336 - وَ رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ مَطَرٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "إِنَّ شُهُودَ اَلزُّورِ يُجْلَدُونَ حَدّاً لَيْسَ لَهُ وَقْتٌ ذَلِكَ إِلَى اَلْإِمَامِ وَ يُطَافُ بِهِمْ حَتَّى يَعْرِفَهُمُ اَلنَّاسُ وَ قَوْلُهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ،
۝  وَ لاٰ تَقْبَلُوا لَهُمْ شَهٰادَةً أَبَداً وَ أُولٰئِكَ هُمُ اَلْفٰاسِقُونَ إِلاَّ اَلَّذِينَ تٰابُوا ۝ "
قُلْتُ بِمَ تُعْرَفُ تَوْبَتُهُ قَالَ "يُكَذِّبُ نَفْسَهُ عَلَى رُءُوسِ اَلْأَشْهَادِ حَيْثُ يُضْرَبُ وَ يَسْتَغْفِرُ رَبَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فَإِنْ هُوَ فَعَلَ ذَلِكَ فَثَمَّ ظَهَرَتْ تَوْبَتُهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3336 - Ali ibn Matar narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, who reported from Abu Abdullah (as), that Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"False witnesses are subjected to a punishment of flogging, the duration of which is determined by the Imam. They are paraded so that people recognize them." Allah (swt), the Almighty, says: 'And do not accept their testimony ever, and those are the defiantly disobedient-except for those who repent.' (Surah An-Nur 24:4-5)
I asked: 'How is their repentance recognized?'
Imam (as) said: 'He must publicly declare his falsehood before the witnesses where he was punished, and seek forgiveness from his Lord (azj), the Almighty. If he does this, then his repentance becomes evident.'"

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on False Testimony and What Has Been Reported Regarding it
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3337 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said: "The speech of a false witness does not end before the judge without him having taken his seat in the Hellfire, and the same applies to one who conceals testimony."
records.arabicText: 3337 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "لاَ يَنْقَضِي كَلاَمُ شَاهِدِ زُورٍ مِنْ بَيْنِ يَدَيِ اَلْحَاكِمِ حَتَّى يَتَبَوَّأَ مَقْعَدَهُ مِنَ اَلنَّارِ وَ كَذَلِكَ مَنْ كَتَمَ اَلشَّهَادَةَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3337 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The speech of a false witness does not end before the judge without him having taken his seat in the Hellfire, and the same applies to one who conceals testimony."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on False Testimony and What Has Been Reported Regarding it
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3338 - Salih bin Mitham narrated from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said: "There is no man who gives false testimony against a Muslim to seize his wealth except that Allah (swt) writes for him a decree to Hell in its place."
records.arabicText: 3338 - وَ رَوَى صَالِحُ بْنُ مِيثَمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "مَا مِنْ رَجُلٍ يَشْهَدُ شَهَادَةَ زُورٍ عَلَى رَجُلٍ مُسْلِمٍ لِيَقْطَعَ مَالَهُ إِلاَّ كَتَبَ اَللَّهُ لَهُ مَكَانَهُ صَكّاً إِلَى اَلنَّارِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3338 - Salih bin Mitham narrated from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no man who gives false testimony against a Muslim to seize his wealth except that Allah (swt) writes for him a decree to Hell in its place."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on False Testimony and What Has Been Reported Regarding it
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3339 - Jamil bin Darraj narrated from someone who informed him about one of the Imams, peace be upon them, who said:
"Regarding witnesses who testify against a man and then retract their testimony-if a judgment has already been passed against the man, the witnesses are held liable for what they testified and must pay compensation. However, if no judgment has been passed, their testimony is discarded, and the witnesses are not required to pay anything."
records.arabicText: 3339 - وَ رَوَى جَمِيلُ بْنُ دَرَّاجٍ عَمَّنْ أَخْبَرَهُ عَنْ أَحَدِهِمَا عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ : "فِي اَلشُّهُودِ إِذَا شَهِدُوا عَلَى رَجُلٍ ثُمَّ رَجَعُوا عَنْ شَهَادَتِهِمْ وَ قَدْ قُضِيَ عَلَى اَلرَّجُلِ ضُمِّنُوا مَا شَهِدُوا بِهِ وَ غُرِّمُوا فَإِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ قُضِيَ طُرِحَتْ شَهَادَتُهُمْ وَ لَمْ يُغَرَّمِ اَلشُّهُودُ شَيْئاً".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3339 - Jamil bin Darraj narrated from someone who informed him about one of the Imams, peace be upon them, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Regarding witnesses who testify against a man and then retract their testimony-if a judgment has already been passed against the man, the witnesses are held liable for what they testified and must pay compensation. However, if no judgment has been passed, their testimony is discarded, and the witnesses are not required to pay anything."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on the Invalidation of the claimant's Right by Administering an Oath Even if They Have Evidence
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3340 - Abdullah bin Abi Ya'fur narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"If the claimant is satisfied with the oath of the defendant regarding his claim, and he requests the defendant to swear, and the defendant swears that there is no right due to the claimant upon him, then the oath nullifies the claimant's right, and he no longer has a claim."
I said: "What if the claimant has a just and valid proof?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes, even if he later presents fifty witnesses after having requested the defendant to swear by Allah (swt), he would have no right, for the oath has invalidated all previous claims for which he had requested the defendant to swear."
records.arabicText: 3340 - رَوَى عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ أَبِي يَعْفُورٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "إِذَا رَضِيَ صَاحِبُ اَلْحَقِّ بِيَمِينِ اَلْمُنْكِرِ لِحَقِّهِ فَاسْتَحْلَفَهُ فَحَلَفَ أَنْ لاَ حَقَّ لَهُ قِبَلَهُ ذَهَبَتِ اَلْيَمِينُ بِحَقِّ اَلْمُدَّعِي وَ لاَ دَعْوَى لَهُ" قُلْتُ وَ إِنْ كَانَتْ لَهُ بَيِّنَةٌ عَادِلَةٌ قَالَ "نَعَمْ وَ إِنْ أَقَامَ بَعْدَ مَا اِسْتَحْلَفَهُ بِاللَّهِ خَمْسِينَ قَسَامَةً مَا كَانَ لَهُ حَقٌّ فَإِنَّ اَلْيَمِينَ قَدْ أَبْطَلَتْ كُلَّ مَا اِدَّعَاهُ قَبْلَهُ مِمَّا قَدِ اِسْتَحْلَفَهُ عَلَيْهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3340 - Abdullah bin Abi Ya'fur narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"If the claimant is satisfied with the oath of the defendant regarding his claim, and he requests the defendant to swear, and the defendant swears that there is no right due to the claimant upon him, then the oath nullifies the claimant's right, and he no longer has a claim."
I said: "What if the claimant has a just and valid proof?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes, even if he later presents fifty witnesses after having requested the defendant to swear by Allah (swt), he would have no right, for the oath has invalidated all previous claims for which he had requested the defendant to swear."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on the Invalidation of the claimant's Right by Administering an Oath Even if They Have Evidence
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3341 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said: "Whoever swears to you by Allah (swt) regarding a right, then believe him, and whoever asks you in the name of Allah (swt), then give him. The oath is for the claimant, and there is no claim for him without it."
records.arabicText: 3341 - قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "مَنْ حَلَفَ لَكُمْ بِاللَّهِ عَلَى حَقٍّ فَصَدِّقُوهُ وَ مَنْ سَأَلَكُمْ بِاللَّهِ فَأَعْطُوهُ ذَهَبَتِ اَلْيَمِينُ بِدَعْوَى اَلْمُدَّعِي وَ لاَ دَعْوَى لَهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3341 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever swears to you by Allah (swt) regarding a right, then believe him, and whoever asks you in the name of Allah (swt), then give him. The oath is for the claimant, and there is no claim for him without it."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on Judging by Reversing the Oath and the Invalidation of the Right Due to Refusal
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3342 - Aban narrated from Jamil, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"If the claimant presents evidence, then no oath is required from him. However, if he does not present evidence and the defendant offers to take an oath, but the claimant refuses, then he has no right to his claim."
records.arabicText: 3342 - رَوَى أَبَانٌ عَنْ جَمِيلٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "إِذَا أَقَامَ اَلْمُدَّعِي اَلْبَيِّنَةَ فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ يَمِينٌ وَ إِنْ لَمْ يُقِمِ اَلْبَيِّنَةَ فَرَدَّ عَلَيْهِ اَلَّذِي اُدُّعِيَ عَلَيْهِ اَلْيَمِينَ فَأَبَى فَلاَ حَقَّ لَهُ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3342 - Aban narrated from Jamil, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If the claimant presents evidence, then no oath is required from him. However, if he does not present evidence and the defendant offers to take an oath, but the claimant refuses, then he has no right to his claim."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on Judging by Administering an Oath to the Claimant Against the Deceased After Presenting Evidence
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3344 - It is narrated by Shu'ayb from Abu Basir, from Abu Abdullah (as), that he mentioned: A group of people came to Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as), disputing ownership of a mule. Both parties presented evidence.
One group claimed that they had raised it at their feeding trough, had neither sold it nor gifted it, and provided witnesses to support their claim.
The other group also claimed that they had raised it at their feeding trough, had neither sold it nor gifted it, and provided their own witnesses.
Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) ruled in favor of the group that had a greater number of witnesses and required them to take an oath to support their claim.
records.arabicText: 3344 - رَوَى شُعَيْبٌ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ ذَكَرَ : "أَنَّ عَلِيّاً عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَتَاهُ قَوْمٌ يَخْتَصِمُونَ فِي بَغْلَةٍ فَقَامَتِ اَلْبَيِّنَةُ لِهَؤُلاَءِ أَنَّهُمْ أَنْتَجُوهَا عَلَى مِذْوَدِهِمْ لَمْ يَبِيعُوا وَ لَمْ يَهَبُوا وَ قَامَتِ اَلْبَيِّنَةُ لِهَؤُلاَءِ أَنَّهُمْ أَنْتَجُوهَا عَلَى مِذْوَدِهِمْ لَمْ يَبِيعُوا وَ لَمْ يَهَبُوا فَقَضَى عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ بِهَا لِأَكْثَرِهِمْ بَيِّنَةً وَ اِسْتَحْلَفَهُمْ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3344 - It is narrated by Shu'ayb from Abu Basir, from Abu Abdullah (as), that he mentioned:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
A group of people came to Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as), disputing ownership of a mule. Both parties presented evidence.
One group claimed that they had raised it at their feeding trough, had neither sold it nor gifted it, and provided witnesses to support their claim.
The other group also claimed that they had raised it at their feeding trough, had neither sold it nor gifted it, and provided their own witnesses.
Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) ruled in favor of the group that had a greater number of witnesses and required them to take an oath to support their claim.
